content.claim: In England, regions with higher special education funding levels award more EHCPs and show higher proportions of children in segregated special schools, suggesting funding-linked allocation rather than purely needs-based assessment
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: Study focused on children with specific genetic differences, not general SEN population
Regional analysis cannot establish causal direction between funding and diagnosis rates
No evidence presented on whether same children would receive support under different funding models
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
